behaviors.description: Missing required key from both config file and environment reports exact dotted path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Low; this can fail entirely inside validation/error-formatting logic once merged config input is known. REAL-INFRA: None required. MOCK-HIDE: Minimal if test feeds merged config with key absent and asserts exact path string. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Call the config validation/loader function with no value for `database.host` in either provided config data or env map, assert it raises/returns an actionable error containing exactly `database.host` (not alias/case variation).

behaviors.description: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` overrides default `~/.config` when resolving config path.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes, if path resolver is tested alone, wiring bugs in startup loader reading process env can be missed. REAL-INFRA: Needs real env + local filesystem path resolution (no network/subprocess). MOCK-HIDE: Mocked path/open calls can mask wrong base directory usage. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp directory, set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to that path, place config at `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool/config.yaml`, ensure no file exists under `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`, run loader startup in-process, and assert it loads from XDG location.

behaviors.description: `TOOL_` environment variable values take precedence over conflicting YAML file values.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes; precedence bugs often occur in wiring between YAML parse, env normalization, and merge order. REAL-INFRA: Requires real env plus file parse; local-only. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking either source can make precedence appear correct even when real loader order is wrong. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Write config file with a known value (e.g., `database.host: filehost`), set matching override env var (e.g., `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`), run loader, assert effective config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax errors surface with detected line number in actionable error message.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: High if parser exceptions are mocked; real parser message/line extraction can differ. REAL-INFRA: Needs actual YAML parser execution (local file/string). MOCK-HIDE: Mocked parser would likely always provide expected line, hiding integration defects in exception handling. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Create malformed YAML with a known bad line (e.g., unmatched bracket on line 4), run loader, assert failure message includes line number `4` (or parser-reported line) and indicates config parse error.

behaviors.description: If config directory is missing at startup, loader creates directory and writes defaults file before continuing.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes; pure mocks can miss mkdir/write ordering and path mistakes. REAL-INFRA: Requires real filesystem side effects (mkdir + file write), but no subprocess/network required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked fs often passes even when permissions/path handling is wrong. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Use an isolated temp home/XDG path with no `tool` config directory, run startup loader, assert directory now exists, defaults file exists at expected path, and loader proceeds to usable config state.
